subject: NVIDIA Cosmos 3 Edge 4B
conflicts: NVIDIA develops the model and the measured NVIDIA hardware, authored the technical report, and published the benchmark tables.
The Hugging Face launch article includes NVIDIA participation and is not an independent evaluation.

claims.claim: NVIDIA released the 4B Cosmos3-Edge base model and the 4B Cosmos3-Edge-Policy-DROID checkpoint on 2026-07-20.

sources.limitations: Revision was pinned from the Hugging Face model API; card content remains NVIDIA-authored.
Revision was pinned from the Hugging Face model API; card content is NVIDIA-authored.

limitations: Availability was inspected through public cards; weights were not downloaded.
conflicts: NVIDIA is the developer and release source.
recheck_triggers: Model card release date or model scope changes
Stable Hugging Face revision becomes available

claims.claim: Cosmos3-Edge is a 4B Mixture-of-Transformers with autoregressive and diffusion paths, and its published Edge interface covers text, image, video, and action inputs and outputs.

sources.limitations: Vendor-authored report; v1 described Edge as a later release.
Family-level audio description exceeds the explicit Edge model-card I/O list.

sources.version: first public Edge-support commit 0334b6f3da2b8519e9c832175c16fd46d32d6f2a; committed 2026-07-20T16:07:51Z; accessed 2026-07-21
sources.limitations: This public snapshot added Edge-specific SFT launchers and updated README.md, inference_benchmarks.md, and cookbooks/cosmos3/generator/action/run_policy_with_cosmos_framework.md. Its parent 703afe0c770997416db50895f82a547b7b2e5b65 has no Cosmos3-Edge entry in README.md; the commit squashes private history, so it establishes the first public repository revision, not the original private authorship date.

This is the inspected current-main snapshot, not the first public Edge revision. It is the direct child of 0334b6f3da2b8519e9c832175c16fd46d32d6f2a and corrects Edge sampling parameters in the Cosmos Framework audiovisual example.

limitations: The model-version summary covers text, image, video, and action across Edge, but the detailed card splits Reasoner video input from Generator text, image, and action input; the code README separately documents video-conditioned generation.
Audio appears in Cosmos 3 family architecture prose but not in the inspected Edge model-card input/output specification.
GitHub has no separately tagged Edge release as of access; first-public provenance is based on the public commit and its parent.
conflicts: All architecture descriptions originate with NVIDIA.
recheck_triggers: Independent architecture inspection
Edge model card adds or removes a modality
NVIDIA publishes a tagged Edge release or changes the pinned Edge code paths after b01cefc00920e8ecb319f15e20ce1eb8850e41ec

claims.claim: Public model weights and implementation material are available under stated OpenMDW-1.1 terms, but the complete training corpus is not public.

sources.limitations: Weights were not downloaded or hashed in this reporting run.

sources.version: first public Edge-support commit 0334b6f3da2b8519e9c832175c16fd46d32d6f2a; committed 2026-07-20T16:07:51Z; accessed 2026-07-21
sources.limitations: The fixed commit exposes Edge documentation, benchmarks, policy execution, and Edge SFT paths, but examples reference the separate cosmos-framework for some training and serving operations; no separate Edge GitHub release tag exists.
Current-main validation is a later snapshot than first publication. It changes Edge example sampling parameters and must not be reported as the first public Edge revision.
License applies to materials actually provided under it and does not guarantee completeness or third-party rights clearance.

limitations: Redistribution must preserve the license and applicable copyright/origin notices.
Users retain responsibility for third-party rights clearance.
The model card describes NVIDIA-owned and public training data only at an aggregate level.
The first public Edge commit is a snapshot of earlier private work, so the public history does not expose the individual pre-publication code revisions.
conflicts: NVIDIA decides which artifacts and training-data details to disclose.
recheck_triggers: License text or repository license changes
Weights or required code become access-restricted
Dataset provenance is published
NVIDIA publishes a tagged Edge release or moves the verified code paths after b01cefc00920e8ecb319f15e20ce1eb8850e41ec

claims.claim: Cosmos3-Edge ranks first on VANTAGE-Bench among similarly sized 4B models.

sources.limitations: States the rank without an independently audited comparator protocol.
Vendor-authored evaluation and broad multi-model table.

limitations: Comparator inclusion, aggregate scoring, and third-party submission validation were not independently verified.
conflicts: NVIDIA provides the model and reports the result.
recheck_triggers: Independent VANTAGE run at a pinned revision
Public leaderboard adds verified submissions
Scoring protocol changes

claims.claim: Post-training Cosmos3-Edge on DROID yields a state-of-the-art RoboLab policy.

sources.limitations: RoboLab result is presented as an image; exact Edge values were not independently machine-read or rerun.
The 39.7% specific-instruction RoboLab result is for Cosmos3-Nano-Policy-DROID 16B, not Edge 4B.

limitations: No independent Edge policy run or variance estimate was found.
conflicts: NVIDIA post-trained the policy and published the benchmark result.
recheck_triggers: Text-form Edge RoboLab ledger with rollout counts appears
Independent pinned-revision RoboLab evaluation

claims.claim: A 32-action Cosmos3-Edge DROID policy meets a 15 Hz control budget on Jetson Thor.

measurement.platform: NVIDIA Jetson AGX Thor T5000, 128 GB, MAXN, 1575 MHz
measurement.request_mode: one request at a time over persistent loopback connection

sources.limitations: Summarizes a 640x360 observation, 32 actions, and Jetson Thor without the detailed platform protocol.
Detailed table uses 640x540 mapped to 544x736, not the launch article's 640x360; only T5000 meets 15 Hz in that table.

limitations: The launch article and detailed card report different observation resolutions.
T4000, T3000, and T2000 entries in the same PyTorch table do not meet the 15 Hz budget.
Control rate is an action-chunk budget, not generated-video frame rate.
conflicts: NVIDIA supplies the hardware, runtime, model, and measurement.
recheck_triggers: NVIDIA reconciles the resolution mismatch
Independent T5000 reproduction
Tail-latency and sustained thermal results are published

claims.claim: Cosmos3-Edge provides high-throughput inference on datacenter and edge NVIDIA platforms.

selected_measurements.platform: Jetson AGX Thor T5000 128 GB MAXN
Jetson AGX Thor T5000 128 GB MAXN
selected_measurements.conditions: preliminary measurements; runtime not comparable with vLLM serving
selected_measurements.text: 1705 prompt tokens; 8717 prefill tok/s; 37.3 decode tok/s; 3.60 s E2E
selected_measurements.image: 911 prompt tokens; 4845 prefill tok/s; 42.6 decode tok/s; 3.17 s E2E
selected_measurements.video: 1263 prompt tokens; 6032 prefill tok/s; 41.8 decode tok/s; 3.25 s E2E

sources.limitations: NVIDIA-run tables; different runtime stacks, workloads, resolution, and concurrency are not directly comparable.

limitations: Generator tables use a single GPU or platform and batch size 1.
T2000 I2V uses 448x256 while T5000/T3000 use 832x480.
Higher aggregate serving throughput at concurrency 64 accompanies much higher request latency.
No power, sustained thermal, tail-latency, or independent cost measurement is included.
conflicts: All selected performance values were measured and published by NVIDIA on NVIDIA hardware.
recheck_triggers: Independent pinned-version hardware benchmark
Sustained power and tail-latency report
Model-card PBR methodology changes

release_gate.reason: The public weights, code, license, architecture, and measurement conditions create clear verification value; performance-superiority language is retained only as attributed vendor claims with adjacent limits.
release_gate.site_exposure_basis: The dedicated aiArticles collection is isolated under content/ai, and /ai routes require a matching approved release record with pinned article and evidence hashes.
